objectives: Deep performance analysis of mx20022 workspace across all crates
Identify and rank optimization opportunities by impact
Provide actionable recommendations with file:line references
constraints: Generated code in mx20022-model/src/generated/ is machine-written via codegen
important_facts: 270,191 lines of generated code across 18 files
6,804 format!-based path allocations in validate_constraints across generated code
4,296 .chars().count() calls in generated code (O(n) per call for ASCII strings)
9,334 total format! calls in generated code (path construction + error messages)
Codegen emitter at emit/validate.rs:78-141 generates format! for every field traversal unconditionally
SEPA charset check at sepa.rs:256 calls is_sepa_charset(&c.to_string()) per character - String alloc per char
IBAN validation at iban.rs:51 allocates String for canonical form on every call
IBAN iban_to_numeric at iban.rs:96 allocates String and calls n.to_string() per letter
xml_scan extract functions allocate String via .to_owned() on every element match
MT parser extract_blocks at parser.rs:69 converts entire input to Vec<char>
ConstraintSet::for_path at constraints.rs:69-71 uses linear scan over Vec
RuleRegistry::with_defaults at rules/mod.rs:108-119 allocates 8 Box<dyn Rule>
MessageId fields are all String (envelope.rs:21-30) for 3-13 char values
All newtypes use pub String (heap alloc) even for 3-char currency codes
Remittance info chunking at mt103_to_pacs008.rs:147-151 collects to Vec<char> then re-collects
parse_tag_value_pairs at parser.rs:344-412 collects chars to Vec then re-collects subslices to String
risks_issues: 270K generated LoC: compile time scales with schema count, no feature-gating
validate_constraints format! allocations are unconditional (even on valid messages with zero violations)

iteration.summary: Performed deep source-level performance analysis of all mx20022 crates. Read all benchmark files, core parse/validate/translate source, codegen emitter, and generated code patterns. Confirmed and expanded prior findings: 6,804 unconditional format! allocations in validate_constraints, 4,296 .chars().count() calls, SEPA per-char String alloc, IBAN unnecessary alloc, MT parser Vec<char> copies, ConstraintSet linear scan. New findings include remittance info double-collect in translation, parse_tag_value_pairs char-by-char alloc pattern, and MessageId using String for tiny fixed-size fields.
outputs_produced: Performance analysis report (this context + user-facing detailed report)
next_actions: Fix codegen to defer format! in validate_constraints until violation found
Replace .chars().count() with .len() in codegen for ASCII-only fields
Add is_sepa_char(c: char) -> bool to eliminate per-char String alloc
Rewrite IBAN canonical form to use stack buffer instead of String
Rewrite MT parser extract_blocks to use byte-level scanning instead of Vec<char>
Index ConstraintSet by path using HashMap
Add benchmarks for validate_constraints, large message parsing, memory usage

mentions.relevance_note: Created the benchmark suites analyzed here
Code quality review may overlap with perf-relevant code patterns
